summary: Comprehensive testing of Cactus card game with HTTP polling (WebSocket replacement). All 15 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend UI fully functional with polling-based real-time updates working correctly.

issues: Turn indicator at top is in white text - could have better contrast for visibility

critical_code_review_comments: Polling implementation is solid - uses refs to track previous state and prevent unnecessary re-renders
Bot auto-play works correctly via execute_bot_turn() called immediately after player action
Session management issues from previous iterations appear to be fixed - login/logout works correctly
context_for_next_testing_agent: HTTP polling successfully replaces WebSockets. Polling intervals: 1.5s in GameRoom lobby, 1s in GameBoard. Bot mode fully working - bot plays automatically after player's turn via execute_bot_turn(). All core game mechanics tested: login, create room, join room, start game, initial card reveal, draw from deck, discard, exchange cards.

features_tested.polling_real_time_lobby: PASSED - GameRoom polls every 1.5s for new players and game state changes
features_tested.polling_real_time_game: PASSED - GameBoard polls every 1s for turn changes and game updates
features_tested.bot_auto_play: PASSED - Bot plays automatically after player's turn without manual refresh
features_tested.initial_card_reveal: PASSED - Player can click to reveal 2 cards at game start
features_tested.draw_from_deck: PASSED - Drawing cards from deck works correctly
features_tested.exchange_card: PASSED - Exchanging drawn card with hand card works
features_tested.session_management: PASSED - Login persists correctly, no unexpected logouts

polling_verification.mechanism: HTTP polling via setInterval with axios GET to /api/game/room/{code}
polling_verification.turn_detection: Uses refs to track previousTurn and detect changes
polling_verification.notification: Toast messages for turn changes and phase changes
